title: Listed companies post record profits around the world
subhead: The number of publicly traded companies reporting  superprofits is multiplying, as are the sums returned to shareholders in the form of dividends or buybacks.

In Hungary, jubilation marks Péter Magyar's inauguration as prime minister
subhead: After securing a decisive victory in April, the pro-European conservative pledged to 'work to ensure the rule of law returns quickly' and, above all, not to repeat the mistakes of his nationalist predecessor, Viktor Orban.

title: Bardella met with German ambassador in a first for the far-right party
subhead: The meeting, which took place in Paris in February, was only revealed on Wednesday by Agence France-Presse. In December, Bardella was received by the US ambassador in Paris. In mid-April, Marine Le Pen met with the Israeli ambassador to France.

title: First French prototypes that extract CO₂ from the air offer promise and uncertainty
subhead: French start-ups and researchers are developing technologies capable of capturing carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere. While promising on paper, these 'negative emissions' solutions remain costly, uncertain and controversial.

title: Women are featured on Cannes's poster, but still sidelined at the festival
subhead: The Cannes Film Festival's choice of a still from 'Thelma & Louise' for its official poster has drawn criticism from the feminist collective 50/50, which points to the persistently low number of female directors in the official selection.

Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
subhead: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

title: Military deterrence has fully entered 'third nuclear age'
subhead: India, Pakistan, Ukraine, North Korea, Iran: The military revolution has also impacted the nuclear weapon debate, notably with the end of transparency and new announcements of increases to global arsenals, including in France.

title: How chicken is taking over France's fast-food market
subhead: The number of fast-food chains dedicated to chicken is booming in France. At the heart of this success is affordable comfort food, offering an unbeatable quantity-for-price ratio at a time when purchasing power is on the decline.
